destinations.evidence: ExecutorRequest-based validators all share the helper that rejects malformed non-empty encrypted secret blobs and non-empty user public keys that are not 65-byte 0x04-prefixed keys.

equivalence_argument: HTTP, LLM, long-running HTTP, FHE, DKMS, persistent-agent, and multimodal ExecutorRequest-derived payload validators call the shared base helper, preserving the crypto-shape invariant at the common validation point.
notes: This mapping is the shared helper underlying invariant 518.
rationale: The primary diff adds shared validate_base_fields, validate_ecies_blob_array, validate_ecies_blob, and validate_user_public_key helpers and wires them into HTTP, LLM, executor, long-running HTTP, and agent validation paths. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Async payload validators that consume ExecutorRequest base fields must reject nonzero encrypted secret blobs shorter than 93 bytes or not starting with 0x04, and must reject nonempty user_public_key values unless they are exactly 65-byte uncompressed SEC1 keys starting with 0x04.
suggested_falsification_tests: For each ExecutorRequest-based precompile validator, submit payloads with empty secrets/key, valid ECIES blob plus uncompressed key, a 3-byte nonempty secret, a secret with 0x02 prefix, a 3-byte user_public_key, and a 65-byte key with 0x02 prefix; assert only the empty/valid cases pass.
summary: Base executor fields validate ECIES and user public key shapes.
test_or_falsification_candidate: For each ExecutorRequest-based validator, submit empty fields, valid 0x04-prefixed ECIES/user keys, short ECIES blobs, wrong ECIES prefixes, short user keys, and wrong user-key prefixes and assert only valid/empty cases pass.
title: Base executor fields validate crypto shapes
